text: A subagent is a specialized assistant that runs in its own context window, with its own system prompt, tools, and permissions.

evidence.quote: A subagent is a specialized assistant that runs in its own context window, with its own system prompt, tools, and permissions.

text: A subagent workflow consists of the main agent calling it, the subagent doing the work in isolation, and returning a summary.

evidence.quote: The main agent calls it, the subagent does the work in isolation, and returns a summary.

text: A subagent is created with a Markdown file and frontmatter containing metadata like name, description, tools, and model.

evidence.quote: You create one with a Markdown file and frontmatter:

text: Claude Code picks up subagents automatically and invokes them when the description matches the task.

evidence.quote: Claude Code picks it up automatically and invokes it when the description matches the task.

text: Subagent files can be saved in different locations to control scope, and when two subagents share the same name, the higher-priority location wins.

evidence.quote: You can save the file in different locations depending on scope. When two subagents share the same name, the higher-priority location wins.

text: The typical subagent locations are .claude/agents/ for team-shared agents and /.claude/agents/ for personal agents.

evidence.quote: For most cases you'll use .claude/agents/ (check it into version control, share with your team) or /.claude/agents/ (personal, available everywhere).

text: Without subagents, every grep, find, ls, and glob call stays in context, and after thirty minutes the context can contain 80k tokens of noise.

evidence.quote: Thirty minutes in, you have 80k tokens of noise you'll never read.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: When Claude compacts the context, important details get lost in the summary.

evidence.quote: Important details get lost in the summary

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The Explore subagent searches the codebase without polluting the main context, firing all grep and find calls in its own window and returning only relevant findings.

evidence.quote: Explore: searches the codebase without polluting your main context. Fires all the grep and find calls in its own window and returns only the relevant findings.

text: The Plan subagent investigates and produces an implementation plan, reading files, understanding the architecture, and returning a step-by-step document.

evidence.quote: Plan: investigates and produces an implementation plan. Reads files, understands the architecture, and returns a step-by-step doc.

text: Instead of 50 tool calls in the main window, subagents return 3 lines with the answer, and the rest is discarded.

evidence.quote: Instead of 50 tool calls in your window, you get 3 lines with the answer. The rest is discarded.

text: By default, a subagent starts with a blank context, which can be limiting when the parent has already built up context tokens.

evidence.quote: By default, a subagent starts with a blank context. Good for cleanliness, bad when you've already invested 100k tokens building understanding of your codebase and you want the subagent to inherit all of it.

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p030.s001`..`p031.s002` around the ref
1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Forking the context copies the parent's context to the subagent at the moment of the fork, and is enabled by setting CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1.

evidence.quote: Forking solves that: the subagent starts with an exact copy of the parent's context at the moment of the fork.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Forked subagents also share the prompt cache prefix with the parent, making children 2-N 10x cheaper on input tokens.

evidence.quote: Shares the prompt cache prefix with the parent (children 2-N are 10x cheaper on input tokens)

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Start with a simple subagent in .claude/agents/ to keep Claude Code context clean during long sessions.

evidence.quote: My recommendation, start with a simple subagent in .claude/agents/. You'll feel the difference on the first long session.
